channel.title: AI Thought Leader Talks (YouTube)
channel.description: Original long-form talks and interviews with AI thought leaders, filtered by Claude.

item.title: [Geoffrey Hinton] The AI Scenario Nobody Wants To Talk About | Geoffrey Hinton (The Diary Of A CEO)
item.author: The Lumen Project
item.description:
The Lumen Project · 27 min
Geoffrey Hinton, the Godfather of AI, explains why the technology he spent his life developing could become one of humanity's greatest risks. From autonomous weapons to AI-driven cyber attacks, and the possibility of machines surpassing human intelligence — a psychological and technological turning point.

item.title: [Channel: Zhang Xiaojun Podcast] A 7-hour marathon interview with Saining Xie: World Models, AMI Labs, Yann LeCun, Fei-Fei Li, and 42
item.author: Zhang Xiaojun Podcast
item.description:
Zhang Xiaojun Podcast · 405 min
Saining Xie in a 7-hour marathon interview. Born 1990, NYU professor, former Google DeepMind and Meta FAIR researcher, co-author of Diffusion Transformers (DiT). With Yann LeCun he co-founded AMI Labs (Advanced Machine Intelligence Labs) — 25 people, $1.03B seed at $3.5B pre-money. Xie calls Silicon Valley 'completely hypnotized' by LLMs, and argues LLMs are a rebellion against the Bitter Lesson, language models' Scaling Laws contain 'water,' and language may be 'poison' for vision. Covers his career, friendships with Kaiming He and拒绝了Ilya twice, Yann LeCun and Fei-Fei Li, world models, and the philosophy of research. Timestamps cover: The Normal One, World Won't Let Me Do Vision, Academic Nomadism, Friendship with Kaiming He, Turning Down Ilya Twice, Memories of Yann LeCun and Fei-Fei Li, Research Taste and the Diamond Sutra, What is a World Model, From Downloading the Internet to Downloading Humanity, Genesis of AMI Labs, 'Silicon Valley is Hypnotized', The Arrogance of Mankind, '42'.

item.title: [Jensen Huang] NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang GTC 2026 Full Keynote
item.author: Yahoo Finance
item.description:
Yahoo Finance · 140 min
NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang's full GTC 2026 keynote. Covers Blackwell/Rubin revenue outlook, the Groq acqui-hire agreement, and AI cloud strategy. Also notes Nebius deal with Meta for up to $27B capacity starting 2027.

item.title: [Topic: OpenAI Talks] Building AI for better healthcare — the OpenAI Podcast Ep. 14
item.author: OpenAI
item.description:
OpenAI · 31 min
OpenAI Head of Health Dr. Nate Gross and Health AI Research lead Karan Singhal discuss how AI can help address the biggest challenges facing healthcare systems worldwide. Covers how OpenAI is training models for sensitive health questions in collaboration with physicians, and how that foundation is unlocking a new generation of tools for patients, clinicians, and healthcare systems. Chapters include: origins of their interest in AI and healthcare, strategy for building AI tools for clinicians, how AI models are trained for health use cases, scoring well on health evals, key challenges deploying AI in healthcare, collaboration with hospitals, practical everyday uses, and feedback from clinicians.

item.title: [Topic: Meta AI Talks] Meta NeuroScientist SHOCKED Me: Scale Alone Won’t Create Consciousness!
item.author: Dr Brian Keating
item.description:
Dr Brian Keating · 51 min
Dr. David Sussillo of Meta Reality Labs explains why scaling transformers alone won't produce conscious AI — and why studying the brain may be the only way forward. Former Google Brain researcher, now at Meta Reality Labs, leads research on RNNs vs transformers debate. Discusses the FORCE algorithm for training RNNs, Meta's EMG wristband and biological signals, Rich Sutton's Bitter Lesson and whether we're locked into a GPU-transformer paradigm, why studying the brain may be the only way out of AI's current limitations. Author of 'Emergence', a memoir about his path from foster care to the frontier of neuroscience and AI research.

item.title: [Channel: Dwarkesh Patel] Dylan Patel — The single biggest bottleneck to scaling AI compute
item.author: Dwarkesh Patel
item.description:
Dwarkesh Patel · 151 min
Dylan Patel, founder of SemiAnalysis, provides a deep dive into the 3 big bottlenecks to scaling AI compute: logic, memory, and power. Walks through the economics of labs, hyperscalers, foundries, and fab equipment manufacturers. Key topics: why an H100 is worth more today than 3 years ago, Nvidia secured TSMC allocation early, Google getting squeezed, ASML as the #1 constraint for AI compute scaling by 2030, the enormous incoming memory crunch, power scaling in the US, China semis competition, TSMC capacity, and robots with Taiwan risk.

item.title: [Channel: Machine Learning Street Talk] When AI Discovers the Next Transformer — Robert Lange
item.author: Machine Learning Street Talk
item.description:
Machine Learning Street Talk · 78 min
Robert Lange, founding researcher at Sakana AI, discusses Shinka Evolve — a framework combining LLMs with evolutionary algorithms for open-ended program search. Core claim: systems like AlphaEvolve need humans to hand them the right problem; Shinka tries to invent new problems automatically. Topics: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search, UCB bandit for adaptive model selection between GPT-5/Sonnet 4.5/Gemini mid-run, evolved load-balancing loss functions for MoE models, agent scaffolds for AIME math benchmarks, and whether automated research pipelines can produce real science or just workshop-level slop.

item.title: [Channel: Zhang Xiaojun Podcast] 133. 对谢赛宁的7小时马拉松访谈：世界模型、逃出硅谷、AMI Labs、两次拒绝Ilya、杨立昆、李飞飞和42
item.author: Zhang Xiaojun Podcast
item.description:
Zhang Xiaojun Podcast · 406 min
Saining Xie discusses world models, exiting Silicon Valley, AMI Labs co-founded with Yann LeCun, declining Ilya twice, and reflections on Fei-Fei Li and Yann LeCun. Xie says 'Silicon Valley is very LLM-pilled — completely hypnotized.' Topics: Research Taste and the Diamond Sutra, What is a World Model, From Downloading the Internet to Downloading Humanity, Genesis of AMI Labs with Yann LeCun, 'Silicon Valley is Hypnotized', The Arrogance of Mankind, and '42'.
